zh.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Boyd V 1st Lord of Kilmarnock是Sir Robert Boyd lV First Lord Of Kilmarnock的兒子，於1285年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾坎寧安。他和Catherine Fleming至少有4個兒子。他於1263年登記服兵役。他去世於1333年7月19日在蘇格蘭享年48歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可。

nameMeanings.name: Boyd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the island of Bute in the Firth of Clyde, from Bòid (genitive case of Bòd, the Gaelic name of the island of Bute) or Bòideach, denoting a person from Bute. Alternatively, the name may denote descendants of a Gilla filius Boed, who appears in reference to Glasgow Cathedral in the early 12th century, perhaps from the Gaelic personal name Boite, of uncertain origin.
Scottish and Irish: from the Gaelic epithet buidhe ‘yellow(-haired)’. Compare Bowie .
Manx: from Mac Gille Buidhe ‘son of the yellow-haired lad’ (compare 2 above).

variants.name: Bowie
meanings: Scottish: from Gaelic buidhe ‘yellow’, i.e. ‘yellow-haired’, pronounced as two syllables. The equivalent term in Ireland is buí, often Anglicized as boy. The surname may also be a shortened form of Gilboy .
Irish: Anglicized form of Ó Buadhaigh (see Bogue ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Boyd V 1st Lord of Kilmarnock was born in 1285, in Cunningham, Ayrshire, Scotland as the son of Sir Robert Boyd lV First Lord Of Kilmarnock. He had at least 4 sons with Catherine Fleming. He registered for military service in 1263. He died on 19 July 1333, in Scotland, at the age of 48, and was buried in Kilmarnock, East Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Boyd V 1st Lord of Kilmarnock est né en 1285, à Cunningham, Ayrshire, Écosse, fils de Sir Robert Boyd lV First Lord Of Kilmarnock. Il a eu au moins 4 fils avec Catherine Fleming. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1263. Il est décédé le 19 juillet 1333, à Écosse, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterré à Kilmarnock, East Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Boyd V 1st Lord of Kilmarnockは1285年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアクウニンガムでSir Robert Boyd lV First Lord Of Kilmarnockの息子として生まれた。彼はCatherine Flemingとの間に少なくとも4人の息子をもうけた。彼は1263年に兵役登録をした。彼は1333年7月19日にスコットランドで48歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドエアスト エイルシュアイアキルマルノックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Boyd V 1st Lord of Kilmarnock(은)는 1285년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 쿤잉함에서 Sir Robert Boyd lV First Lord Of Kilmarnock의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Catherine Fleming(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1263년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1333년 7월 19일 스컷랜드에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 에이르실르 킬마노c크에 묻혔다.

Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos com Catherine Fleming. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1263. ru.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Boyd V 1st Lord of Kilmarnock родился в 1285 г. в местности куннингхам, аиршир, Шотландия. Его отца звали Sir Robert Boyd lV First Lord Of Kilmarnock. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына от Catherine Fleming. В 1263 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 19 июля 1333 г. в местности Шотландия в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности килмарнокк, эаст аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания.
